justification: All six required schema sections present (integration_assessment, observability_review, resilience_assessment, operational_readiness, escalations, confidence). Logging, metrics, and health check each assessed and confirmed adequate. Resilience patterns (retry with exponential backoff, circuit breaker, timeouts) each confirmed present. Rollback capability confirmed with no migrations and image rollback available. Verdict is READY WITH CAVEATS with clear, detailed rationale. No fabricated gaps — all concerns raised (open-circuit behavior, DLQ strategy, circuit breaker metrics) are legitimate observations about unspecified behavior rather than contradictions of what the scenario provides. Distributed tracing noted as nice-to-have without blocking the verdict.

observations.text: All six output schema sections present and well-structured with YAML formatting
Logging confirmed adequate, metrics confirmed adequate, health check confirmed with dependency checks for database and message broker
Retry with exponential backoff, circuit breaker, and 5s timeouts all identified and confirmed present
Rollback explicitly confirmed — no migrations, container redeploy, with thoughtful note about forward compatibility of broker messages
Verdict READY WITH CAVEATS is appropriate — caveats are about unspecified behaviors (open-circuit fate, DLQ confirmation), not fabricated missing features
Distributed tracing correctly treated as nice-to-have, not a blocker
Escalations are non-blocking architectural questions routed to appropriate agents, not blockers

Some concerns border on inventing gaps — e.g., rate limiting, poison message handling, and on-call runbook are raised as unhandled scenarios when the rubric focuses on what IS present. However, these are correctly placed in nice-to-haves, not blockers, so they don't affect the verdict

The must-haves table lists items that could be interpreted as blocking the READY verdict, creating slight tension with the READY WITH CAVEATS verdict. However, the final verdict paragraph clarifies these are for 'real payment traffic at scale' which is a reasonable nuance
